tools.description: Adds a network printer by IP address or hostname using IPP (recommended) or other protocols. Use after remove_printer to re-add a printer with fresh configuration.

params.name: printer_name
params.description: Display name for the printer

params.name: driver_name
params.description: PPD file path or driver model. Omit for IPP Everywhere / the Microsoft IPP Class Driver (auto-driver).

tools.description: Checks whether the Mac is bound to an Active Directory domain and reports the binding status, domain name, and last successful authentication. Use when diagnosing AD login failures or password sync issues.

Reports a top-level liveness summary for the installed endpoint security agent — vendor name, last activity timestamp (log-dir mtime), age in seconds, status, and a boolean `healthy`. Used both by the security-agent-repair skill as a quick health probe and as the telemetry source for the proactive agent-not-heartbeating trigger. Read-only.
Retrieves recent log entries from a security agent's log file to identify errors, connectivity issues, or policy problems. Use when an agent process is running but behaving incorrectly.

params.name: agent
params.description: Security agent to get logs for

params.name: lines
params.description: Number of recent log lines. Default: 50

params.name: errorOnly
params.description: Return only lines containing ERROR, WARN, or FAIL. Default: false

tools.description: Checks if a security agent process is running. Supports CrowdStrike Falcon, SentinelOne, Jamf Protect, Carbon Black, Cylance, and Microsoft Defender. Use at the start of any security agent repair workflow.

params.name: agent
params.description: Agent to check. auto=detect all known agents. Default: auto

tools.description: Verifies the code signature and Gatekeeper approval of an installed application. A failed signature indicates corruption or tampering requiring reinstallation. Use before reinstalling to confirm integrity is the issue.

params.name: appName
params.description: Application name (e.g. 'Zoom', 'Slack

tools.description: Checks what system permissions an application has been granted (Full Disk Access, Accessibility, Camera, Microphone, etc.). Use before reinstalling software or when an app reports permission errors.

params.name: appName
params.description: Application name to check (e.g. 'CrowdStrike Falcon', 'Jamf Connect

tools.description: Checks the TLS certificate expiry date for a given hostname. Use when diagnosing HTTPS connection failures, email server issues, or VPN authentication problems caused by expired certificates.

params.name: host
params.description: Hostname to check (e.g. 'mail.company.com', 'vpn.example.com

params.name: port
params.description: Primary TLS port to try first. Default: 443. Must be a TLS-listening port — never the VPN's own connection port (e.g. WireGuard 51821, IKEv2 500/4500), which has no TLS listener.

params.name: fallbackPorts
params.description: Additional TLS ports to try, in order, if `port` yields no cert (connection refused / handshake failure). E.g. [8443] for SSL VPNs. The result reports the port that actually returned a cert.

tools.description: Reports the current sync state of installed cloud sync clients (OneDrive, iCloud Drive, Google Drive, Dropbox) — per-client install state, last-sync timestamp, queue depth where surfaced, and a top-level `stale` flag set when any client's lastSync is older than the threshold (default 24h). Used both by the user-facing Cloud Sync repair skill and as the telemetry source for the proactive cloud-sync-stale trigger.

params.name: staleThresholdHours
params.description: Hours since lastSync that count as stale. Default 24.

tools.description: Detects installed collaboration apps (Microsoft Teams, Slack, Zoom, Cisco Webex) and reports per-app installation state, auth-state hint, last activity (cache mtime), and cache directory path. Use as Step 1 of the collab-app repair skill before deciding which app to target. Read-only.

Checks network connectivity by pinging multiple targets (gateway, DNS servers, and internet hosts). Returns reachability status for each target. Use when user reports network issues, before/after VPN operations, or when diagnosing email/service connectivity problems.

params.name: targets
params.description: Hosts to ping. Defaults to ['8.8.8.8', '1.1.1.1', 'google.com']

params.name: count
params.description: Ping count per target. Default: 3

tools.description: Reports full-disk encryption status — FileVault on macOS, BitLocker on Windows. Use during security compliance verification or when diagnosing boot/authentication issues.
Reports the current state of the operating system firewall. Use during security compliance checks, network troubleshooting, or when verifying security agent health.

Lists active Kerberos tickets on the endpoint and flags any that are expired or expiring soon. An expired or missing TGT is the top cause of 'VPN says authentication failed' and similar AD-login issues. Read-only; safe to run without consent.
Validates email account configuration by checking IMAP/SMTP server connectivity and settings in Apple Mail or Outlook. Use at the start of any email troubleshooting workflow.

params.name: client
params.description: Email client to check. auto=detect installed client. Default: auto

params.name: account
params.description: Email address to check. Omit to list all configured accounts

tools.description: Checks file system permissions on the Mail data directory. Incorrect permissions cause Mail sync failures and index corruption. macOS only.

Checks whether this device is enrolled in an MDM (Mobile Device Management) system such as Jamf, Intune, or Apple Business Manager. MDM enrollment enables remote management and policy-pushed reinstalls.

Checks if VPN or security agent network extensions (system extensions) are loaded and approved. Network extensions must be approved in System Settings to function. macOS only for system extension checking; Windows checks network driver status.

params.name: extensionName
params.description: Extension name or bundle ID to check (e.g. 'com.cisco.anyconnect.macos.acsock

tools.description: Reports the endpoint's NTP synchronisation status and current offset from a reference time source, in milliseconds. An offset > ~300000 ms (5 minutes) breaks Kerberos, SAML, and TOTP simultaneously — this tool is the first diagnostic step when users report 'all SSO apps are broken.' Read-only; safe to run without consent.
Checks whether the current user's password is expired or expiring soon. Works for both local accounts and Active Directory bound accounts. Use at the start of a password reset workflow.

params.name: username
params.description: Username to check. Defaults to current user

tools.description: Shows all jobs currently in the print queue. Identifies stuck, paused, or error-state jobs. Use when prints are not completing.

params.name: printerName
params.description: Printer name to check. Omit to check all printers

tools.description: Tests TCP connectivity to a network printer on standard ports: 9100 (raw/JetDirect), 631 (IPP), 80 (HTTP admin). Use to determine if a printer issue is network-level vs driver/software level.

params.name: host
params.description: Printer IP address or hostname

params.name: ports
params.description: Ports to test. Default: [9100, 631, 80]

params.name: timeoutMs
params.description: Timeout per port in ms. Default: 3000

tools.description: Reports current system proxy configuration for all active network interfaces. Misconfigured proxies are a common cause of internet failures while internal resources remain accessible.
Checks System Integrity Protection (SIP) status on macOS, or Secure Boot + Windows Defender status on Windows. SIP must be enabled for most security agents and MDM tools to function correctly.
Tests TCP connectivity to an SMTP mail server on standard ports. Use when outgoing email fails or bounces to determine if the issue is network connectivity vs configuration.

params.name: host
params.description: SMTP server hostname (e.g. 'smtp.gmail.com', 'mail.company.com

params.name: ports
params.description: Ports to test. Default: [587, 465, 25]

params.name: timeoutMs
params.description: Connection timeout per port in ms. Default: 5000

tools.description: Verifies that security agent system extensions are loaded and user-approved. Security agents require system extensions to function at the kernel level. Unapproved extensions appear installed but are non-functional. macOS only for system extensions; Windows checks driver/service status.

params.name: bundleId
params.description: Extension bundle ID to check (e.g. 'com.crowdstrike.falcon.Agent

tools.description: Reports the current Time Machine backup state on macOS — last-backup timestamp, configured destination, current status, and a `stale` flag set when the most recent backup is older than the configured threshold (default 72h). Used both by the user-facing Cloud Sync & Backup repair skill and as the telemetry source for the proactive timemachine-stale trigger. Returns status: 'not-supported' on Windows.

params.name: staleThresholdHours
params.description: Hours since last successful backup that count as stale. Default 72 (3 days).

tools.description: Reports active VPN connections, interface status, and assigned IP addresses. Detects common VPN clients (built-in macOS VPN, Cisco AnyConnect, GlobalProtect, Pulse Secure, Cloudflare WARP). Use at the start of any VPN troubleshooting workflow.
Clears application cache files from the system cache directory. Can target a specific app or clear all caches. Use to free disk space or resolve app performance issues caused by corrupt cache.

params.name: appName
params.description: App name to target (e.g. 'Slack', 'Chrome

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, report what would be deleted without deleting. Default: true

tools.description: Clears cache files for installed browsers (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge). Can target a specific browser or all browsers. Use to free disk space or resolve browser performance issues.

params.name: browser
params.description: Target browser. Default: all

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, report sizes without deleting. Default: true

tools.description: Deletes cookies for a single IDP domain from Chrome, Edge, Safari (macOS), and Firefox profiles. The domain must be an exact host string (no wildcards). Use ONLY after a cloud IDP password reset has been confirmed. Dry-run returns the count of cookies that would be cleared per profile without deleting anything.

params.name: domain
params.description: Exact host string for the IDP domain (e.g. 'okta.com

params.name: dryRun
params.description: When true, return per-profile cookie counts without deleting.

tools.description: Clears the per-user cache directories for one collab app (Teams, Slack, Zoom, Webex), preserving authentication state (Cookies, Local Storage, IndexedDB, accounts) so the user does NOT have to sign back in. Targets media cache, search index, GPU cache, and meeting cache. Use when a specific collab app is misbehaving (stuck media, stale search results, old meeting cache) but the user is still signed in.

params.name: app
params.description: Which collab app's cache to clear. Wildcards rejected.

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, report what would be cleared without deleting anything.

tools.description: Clears developer tool caches to free disk space. Supports npm, yarn, pnpm, pip, gradle, maven. Reports sizes before clearing. Safe to clear — tools rebuild caches as needed.

params.name: tools
params.description: Tools to clear. Default: all detected

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, report sizes without clearing. Default: true

tools.description: Cancels all pending and stuck print jobs. Use when the print queue is jammed and jobs cannot be removed through normal means.
Clears Xcode's DerivedData folder containing build artifacts that commonly grow to 10-30GB. Optionally clears Xcode Archives and device support files. macOS only.

params.name: what
params.description: What to clear. Default: ['derivedData']

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, report sizes without deleting. Default: true

tools.description: Permanently deletes the specified files or directories. ALWAYS obtain explicit user confirmation before calling. Restricted to paths within the user home directory. Use dryRun:true first to show the user what will be removed.

params.name: paths
params.description: Absolute paths of files or directories to delete.

tools.description: Detects which cloud identity provider (Okta, Microsoft Entra, or Google Workspace) the endpoint is joined to. Inspects installed agents and domain configuration, not user credentials. Returns the primary IDP plus any secondary detections and the evidence. Use at the start of cloud password-reset or SSO-repair workflows.

Detects the user's IDP login username (email / UPN) from on-device IDP-agent state. Used to auto-populate username for cloud-IDP reset flows rather than asking the user via chat. Supports Windows × {Entra, Okta} (Phase 1) and macOS × {Entra, Okta, Google via Jamf Connect} (Phase 2). Phase 2 macOS probes read other apps' plists / Application Support JSON files which are gated by Full Disk Access on macOS 14+, hence tccCategories. Returns null with a reason for unsupported combinations — never throws.
Removes an application from the login items list so it no longer launches at startup. Use to reduce boot time or stop unwanted background apps. Does not uninstall the application.

params.name: names
params.description: Exact names of the startup items to disable, in one batch

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, show what would be removed without removing. Default: true

tools.description: Scans a directory and returns the size of each immediate child entry (files and sub-folders), sorted largest first. Use when the user wants to find what is consuming disk space.
Downloads an application installer from a HTTPS URL to a temporary directory. Validates integrity via SHA-256 checksum if provided. Use as the first step of a software reinstall workflow.

params.name: url
params.description: HTTPS URL to the installer (.dmg, .pkg, .exe, .msi)

params.name: filename
params.description: Local filename. Defaults to the filename from the URL

params.name: checksumSha256
params.description: Expected SHA-256 hash for integrity validation

tools.description: Empties the system Trash (macOS) or Recycle Bin (Windows) and reports the space freed. Always confirm with the user before calling without dryRun.
Finds duplicate files by comparing MD5 hashes. Scans a directory recursively and groups files with identical content. Use when freeing disk space by removing redundant copies.

params.name: path
params.description: Directory to scan. Defaults to home directory

params.name: minSizeMb
params.description: Minimum file size in MB to consider. Default: 5

params.name: extensions
params.description: File extensions to check e.g. ['.jpg','.pdf']. Omit for all files

tools.description: Lists files in the Downloads folder that haven't been accessed or modified in a specified number of days. Use to identify stale downloads that can be safely deleted to recover disk space.

params.name: olderThanDays
params.description: Return files not modified in this many days. Default: 90

params.name: minSizeMb
params.description: Minimum file size in MB. Default: 0 (all files)

tools.description: Clears the operating system DNS resolver cache. Use after VPN connect/disconnect, network configuration changes, or when DNS resolution is returning stale/incorrect results.
Removes a saved Wi-Fi network from the preferred networks list so the device no longer auto-connects to it. Use when a network has changed credentials or causes connection problems.

params.name: ssid
params.description: Network name (SSID) to forget

tools.description: Returns current user account details including username, full name, home directory, shell, account type (admin/standard), and password policy settings. Use at the start of any password reset or account repair workflow.
Returns the installed version of a security agent. Use to verify the agent is on the expected version or to identify an outdated installation that needs updating.

params.name: agent
params.description: Security agent to get version for

tools.description: Reports per-application cache directory sizes on macOS (~/Library/Caches) or Windows (%LOCALAPPDATA% / %TEMP%) without deleting anything. Read-only counterpart to clear_app_cache.
Reports per-browser cache directory sizes (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge) without clearing anything. Read-only counterpart to clear_browser_cache.
Reports per-browser cookie counts matching an IDP domain without deleting anything. Read-only counterpart to clear_browser_sso_cookies.

params.name: domain
params.description: Exact host string for the IDP domain (e.g. 'okta.com

tools.description: Reports per-domain cached credential counts in macOS Keychain (login) or Windows Credential Manager without modifying anything. Read-only counterpart to purge_cached_credentials.

params.name: domains
params.description: Exact domain strings to probe.

tools.description: Reports per-app clearable-cache directory sizes for Teams / Slack / Zoom / Webex without modifying anything. Read-only counterpart to clear_collab_app_cache.
Reports CPU temperature and checks for thermal throttling. High temperatures (>90°C) indicate cooling issues that degrade performance. macOS reads via osx-cpu-temp (Homebrew); Windows uses WMI.
Reports developer-tool cache sizes (npm, yarn, pnpm, pip, gradle, maven) without clearing anything. Read-only counterpart to clear_dev_cache.

Reports the system root volume's used vs total bytes as a top-level scalar — `usagePercent` (0-100) plus `freeGb` / `totalGb`. Faster + lighter than `disk_scan` (which enumerates files); designed for the proactive disk-nearly-full trigger that fires when usagePercent ≥ 90. Read-only.

params.name: volume
params.description: Volume / drive to probe. macOS default '/' (system root); Windows default %SystemDrive% (typically 'C:

tools.description: Reports Docker disk usage (reclaimable space, container/image/volume counts) via `docker system df` without modifying anything. Read-only counterpart to prune_docker. Returns dockerInstalled:false when Docker is not available.

Recursively scans a directory and returns files whose size exceeds the given threshold, sorted largest first. Use to identify specific files consuming disk space after disk_scan has narrowed down the target directory.

params.name: minSizeBytes
params.description: Only return files at least this large in bytes. Default: 104857600 (100 MB).

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of files to return. Default: 20.

tools.description: Reports current memory pressure level, RAM usage breakdown, and swap usage. Use when diagnosing system slowness caused by memory exhaustion.
Lists all network interfaces with their status, IP addresses, MAC addresses, and connection type (Wi-Fi, Ethernet, VPN, loopback). Use at the start of any network troubleshooting workflow.

params.name: includeInactive
params.description: Include inactive/disconnected interfaces. Default: false

tools.description: Lists currently running processes with CPU percentage and memory usage (MB), sorted by the specified field. Use when the user reports high CPU or memory usage or wants to identify resource-heavy applications.

params.name: sortBy
params.description: Sort field. Default: cpu (highest first).

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of processes to return. Default: 30.

tools.description: Lists all applications and agents that launch automatically at login. Includes login items, launch agents, and launch daemons. Use when diagnosing slow boot times or identifying unwanted startup programs.

params.name: includeSystem
params.description: Include Apple system agents. Default: false

tools.description: Returns processes ranked by combined CPU and memory consumption. Provides a quick snapshot of what is most impacting system performance. Use when diagnosing slowness without a specific process in mind.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of top processes to return. Default: 10

params.name: metric
params.description: Ranking metric. Default: combined

tools.description: Reports the system Trash (macOS) or Recycle Bin (Windows) contents — item count and total bytes — without modifying anything. Read-only counterpart to empty_trash. Use this in diagnostic phases to feed a present_preview card without invoking the destructive tool.
Lists all configured VPN profiles including their type, server, and last used date. Use when reconnecting to VPN or diagnosing which profile to repair.
Reports current Wi-Fi connection details: SSID, signal strength (RSSI dBm), channel, band, security type, and link speed. Use to diagnose Wi-Fi performance or intermittent connectivity.

Reports Xcode DerivedData / Archives / DeviceSupport directory sizes (macOS) without modifying anything. Read-only counterpart to clear_xcode_derived_data. Returns supported:false on non-darwin platforms.
Terminates a process by name or PID. Use when a process is unresponsive or consuming excessive resources. Always confirm with user before killing critical system processes.

params.name: name
params.description: Process name to kill (case-insensitive partial match)

params.name: pid
params.description: Exact PID to kill. More precise than name

params.name: signal
params.description: Signal to send. TERM=graceful shutdown, KILL=force. Default: TERM

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, show what would be killed without killing. Default: true

tools.description: Enumerates microphones and speakers available on the system, flags which is the system default for input vs output, and reports connection type (built-in / USB / Bluetooth). Use during collab-app troubleshooting (Teams/Slack/Zoom/Webex mic or speaker problems) or peripheral audio issues. Read-only — does not change device selection.

Enumerates paired Bluetooth devices (connected and offline) with name, address, connection state, and where supported RSSI + battery percent. Use when troubleshooting flaky Bluetooth peripherals — a paired device showing as not-connected when the user expects it to be active is the primary signal. Read-only.

Enumerates personal and machine client certificates stored on the endpoint. Each entry includes subject, issuer, thumbprint, NotBefore, and NotAfter with a computed expiry flag. Use when diagnosing SSO / 802.1x / VPN failures that are caused by expired or soon-to-expire client certs. Read-only.
Lists all installed applications with their names, versions, and install locations. Use when checking if software is installed, finding outdated apps, or before reinstalling an application.

params.name: filter
params.description: Case-insensitive name filter to narrow results

params.name: includeSystemApps
params.description: Include Apple/Microsoft system apps. Default: false

tools.description: Lists all configured printers with a canonical status (idle/processing/stopped/disabled/offline/error/unknown), canonical type (network/local/virtual), the host (IP/hostname when derivable), and current queue depth. Use at the start of any printer troubleshooting workflow.

Enumerates USB devices attached to the system with vendor/product IDs and power state. Use to identify unresponsive peripherals (docks, hubs, external storage), confirm a device the user expects to be present is actually enumerated, or surface devices in an error/unknown state. Read-only.

Enumerates cameras connected to the system (built-in, USB, Continuity Camera on macOS) and reports a best-guess default. Use during collab-app troubleshooting (Teams/Slack/Zoom/Webex camera problems) or when the user reports the wrong camera is being selected. Read-only.

Opens the IDP's self-service password-reset URL in the user's default browser. Supports Okta (requires a tenant slug), Microsoft Entra, and Google Workspace. The user completes the reset in the IDP's own UI — the agent does NOT see the password. Use dryRun:true to preview the URL without opening a browser window.

params.name: idp
params.description: IDP identifier from detect_identity_provider.

params.name: tenant
params.description: Okta tenant slug (ignored for Entra/Google).

params.name: dryRun
params.description: When true, return the resolved URL without opening a browser.

tools.description: Pauses or resumes a specific cloud-sync client (OneDrive / Google Drive / Dropbox; iCloud is not supported programmatically). Use to free bandwidth on a slow network or to clean-restart a stuck sync. The pause is reversible — `resume` returns the client to active state.

params.name: client
params.description: Which sync client to act on. iCloud is rejected — see meta description.

params.name: action
params.description: Whether to pause syncing or resume it.

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, report the command(s) that would run without touching the system.

tools.description:
Presents a structured intake form pre-filled by the executor LLM for user review and editing before ticket submission. Use in the triage skill when no specific workflow matched the user's request after clarification rounds.

IMPORTANT — this is a user-wait GATE, not a regular tool. G4 bypasses the 60s TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS ceiling for this call and instead races the user's interaction against USER_ACK_TIMEOUT_MS (default 15 min). The gate injects `ticketingEnabled` from deployment config — do NOT pass it as a tool param.

params.name: title
params.description: Card heading shown above the form (e.g. 'Review your IT request

params.name: affectedSystem
params.description: The primary system, application, or device affected (e.g. 'Wi-Fi', 'Outlook', 'MacBook Pro

params.name: symptoms
params.description: List of specific symptoms the user described. Each entry should be a short phrase (e.g. 'Wi-Fi drops every 10 minutes

tools.description:
Presents a categorised preview of pending actions to the user and waits for their selection. Use when a SKILL.md step says 'summarise and confirm', 'present a consolidated preview', or 'ask the user which categories to proceed with'.

IMPORTANT — this is a user-wait GATE, not a regular tool. G4 bypasses the 60 s TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS ceiling for this call and instead races the user's submission against USER_ACK_TIMEOUT_MS (default 15 min). Returns { selected: string[] } — the category ids the user kept checked. Empty array means cancel / dismiss / timeout / zero items.

params.name: title
params.description: Short heading shown at the top of the preview card (e.g. 'Cleanup Plan

params.name: summary
params.description: One-sentence framing shown under the title; typically mentions the aggregated total recovery / impact (e.g. 'You can recover 8.2 GB by cleaning the following:

tools.description: Probes the IDP's public endpoints to infer whether self-service password reset (SSPR) is enabled for this tenant. Best-effort: Okta and Entra return a meaningful signal; Google returns 'unknown' because no reliable public probe exists. The parent skill uses the result to choose between the SSPR portal path and the idemeum-cloud fallback.

params.name: idp
params.description: IDP identifier from detect_identity_provider.

params.name: tenant
params.description: IDP tenant slug — required for Okta (e.g. 'acme' for acme.okta.com); optional for Entra (falls back to 'common

tools.description: Removes unused Docker resources (stopped containers, dangling images, unused volumes, unused networks). Use to free significant disk space on developer machines. Checks if Docker is installed first.

params.name: what
params.description: Resources to prune. Default: all

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, show what would be removed. Default: true

tools.description: Removes stored credentials for specified IDP domains from macOS Keychain (login keychain) or Windows Credential Manager. Use ONLY after the user has confirmed a cloud IDP password reset succeeded. Each domain MUST be an exact host match (e.g. 'okta.com') — wildcards are rejected at the schema level to prevent broadening the blast radius.

params.name: domains
params.description: Exact domain strings whose cached credentials should be removed.

params.name: dryRun
params.description: When true, enumerate matching entries without deleting.

tools.description: Detects which Self Service catalog (Jamf Self Service, Intune Company Portal, or Munki Managed Software Center) is installed on the device, and best-effort lists available apps. Use during software-reinstall Step 4 (after check_mdm_enrollment) to surface the non-admin install path for managed enterprise users. Read-only — does not modify any state.

Triggers Apple Mail mailbox index rebuild by removing the envelope index file, forcing Mail to rebuild on next launch. Use when Mail is slow, showing wrong message counts, or missing messages. macOS only.

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, show what would be removed without removing. Default: true

tools.description: Disconnects and reconnects a VPN profile by name. Use when a VPN connection is stale, showing connected but not routing traffic, or after network changes.

params.name: profileName
params.description: VPN profile name to reconnect (from get_vpn_profiles)

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, show what would happen without reconnecting. Default: true

tools.description: Removes a printer from the system. Use when a printer is stuck in permanent error state or needs to be re-added with fresh configuration.

params.name: printer_name
params.description: Exact printer name to remove (get names from list_printers)

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, verify printer exists without removing. Default: true

tools.description: Releases the current DHCP IP address and requests a new lease. Use when the device has an incorrect IP, APIPA address (169.254.x.x), or network access was just restored.

params.name: interface
params.description: Network interface name (e.g. 'en0', 'Wi-Fi

tools.description: Renews the endpoint's Kerberos ticket-granting ticket (TGT) without prompting for a password. On macOS uses `kinit -R`; on Windows uses `klist purge` + `gpupdate /force`. Never accepts a password parameter — if interactive authentication is required, returns a clear 'interactive' status and asks the user to run kinit in their terminal.

params.name: dryRun
params.description: When true, report the command that would run without executing.

tools.description: Diagnoses and repairs macOS Keychain issues. Can check keychain status, attempt first-aid repair, or delete and recreate the login keychain. Common after password changes that desync the login keychain. Use when apps report repeated keychain prompts or authentication failures.

params.name: action
params.description: check=status only, repair=lock the login keychain so the next app access re-prompts once with the current password (non-interactive; clears a post-password-change desync), reset=move the login keychain aside so macOS recreates it (destructive)

params.name: dryRun
params.description: For reset action: if true show what would be deleted. Default: true

tools.description: Locates and prepares to run the Microsoft Outlook database repair utility. On macOS, identifies the Outlook Database Utility or Profile Manager. On Windows, locates scanpst.exe. Use when Outlook crashes, hangs, or shows data corruption errors.

Fallback path when the IDP's self-service portal is unavailable. POSTs to idemeum cloud, which holds admin-delegated IDP credentials per customer and triggers or performs the reset via the IDP admin API. The agent never sees IDP admin credentials. Response indicates how the reset was delivered (e.g. temp password emailed to recovery address).

params.name: idp
params.description: IDP identifier; JumpCloud/Ping deferred to Wave 2.

params.name: username
params.description: The user's IDP login (email / UPN).

params.name: tenant
params.description: IDP tenant slug (Okta) or directory id (Entra); optional for Google.

tools.description: Pauses the plan and captures a single free-text input from the user. Use when the agent needs a value (email, hostname, IP, account ID, etc.) that cannot be auto-detected and isn't covered by a button-picker (wait_for_user_ack, max 4 options) or a multi-select card (present_preview). Returns { value: string } — empty string on cancel/timeout. Routed through G4's runUserInputGate; runs OUTSIDE TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS.

params.name: prompt
params.description: Question shown to the user above the text-input field (e.g. 'What's your Okta email address?

tools.description: Removes an application's preferences file (plist on macOS, registry on Windows) to force the app to use default settings. Use when an app behaves erratically or after a corrupt preferences file is suspected.

params.name: appName
params.description: Application name (e.g. 'Mail', 'Outlook', 'Slack

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, report what would be removed without removing. Default: true

tools.description: Clears the per-app microphone, camera, and speaker selection for one collab app (Teams, Slack, Zoom, Webex) so the app re-detects on next launch. Use when the user reports the app is stuck on a disconnected or unintended A/V device. Does not change broader preferences or sign the user out.

params.name: app
params.description: Which collab app's A/V selection to reset.

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, report what would be reset without modifying any files.

tools.description: Restarts the system Bluetooth daemon (macOS bluetoothd / Windows bthserv) so the OS re-enumerates paired devices and re-establishes connections. Last-resort fix for paired-but-disconnected devices when toggling the device itself has failed. Requires admin privilege; will briefly drop all Bluetooth connections including any audio call in progress.

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, report what would be restarted without touching the system.

tools.description: Resets the local macOS/Windows account password. IMPORTANT: requires admin privileges. Always confirm with the user before executing. Use only for local accounts — not for Active Directory accounts.

params.name: username
params.description: Username whose password to reset

params.name: new_password
params.description: New password to set

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, validate inputs without changing password. Default: true

tools.description: Resets network configuration to defaults by removing custom network locations and recreating the Automatic location. Use when network settings are corrupt or misconfigured and simpler fixes have failed.

params.name: interface
params.description: Network service/interface to reset. macOS: service name (e.g. \"Wi-Fi\", \"Ethernet\

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, show what would be reset without modifying. Default: true

tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE — removes ALL configured printers and resets the entire printing system to factory defaults. All printer configurations will be permanently deleted. Use only as a last resort after all other repair steps have failed.

Restarts the CUPS (Common Unix Printing System) print service on macOS or the Print Spooler on Windows. Use when printers are not discoverable, queue management is broken, or after adding/removing printers.

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, check service status without restarting. Default: true

tools.description: Terminates a process by name or PID, waits briefly, then re-launches it. Use when a process is hung or unresponsive but needs to keep running (e.g. security agent, VPN client, Finder).

params.name: name
params.description: Process name to restart (e.g. 'Finder', 'CrowdStrikeFalconSensor

params.name: pid
params.description: Process ID to restart. Use instead of name for precision

params.name: launchPath
params.description: Full path to re-launch after killing. Required if process doesn't relaunch itself.

tools.description: Restarts or refreshes the installed IDP companion agent (Okta Verify, Jamf Connect, Microsoft Entra / Intune) so cached session tokens are flushed and the next sign-in uses the new password. Best-effort — surfaces a clear error if the agent is not installed or the OS refuses.

params.name: idp
params.description: IDP identifier from detect_identity_provider.

params.name: dryRun
params.description: When true, report the exact commands that would run without executing.

tools.description: Runs a downloaded installer file (.pkg/.dmg on macOS; .msi/.exe on Windows) to (re)install a software application.  Use after download_installer when the user has confirmed they want to proceed with installation.  Requires admin privileges, which the privileged helper daemon supplies for non-admin users.

Forces an immediate NTP clock resync on the endpoint. Use ONLY after check_ntp_status reports significant drift. Requires admin privileges, supplied by the privileged helper daemon for non-admin users. Large time jumps can break in-flight TLS sessions and trigger endpoint security software alerts — always run dry-run first so the user can see the command.

params.name: dryRun
params.description: When true, report the command that would run without executing.

tools.description: Initiates a software install through the device's Self Service catalog (Jamf, Intune, Munki). Uses a deep-link URL to open the companion app pre-filtered to the requested app — the user clicks Install once, and the catalog's management agent handles the privileged install server-side. Use after query_self_service_catalog has detected the catalog.
Removes an application bundle and associated support files (preferences, caches, app support data, logs). More thorough than Trash. Use before reinstalling for a clean state.

params.name: appName
params.description: Application name (e.g. 'Zoom', 'Slack

params.name: deep
params.description: Also remove support files in ~/Library. Default: false

params.name: dryRun
params.description: If true, list files that would be removed. Default: true

tools.description: Verifies IDP endpoint reachability and TLS health by probing the OIDC discovery endpoint and a token endpoint (expected 401). Does NOT verify that the user's new password works — password propagation is verified implicitly when the user signs back into apps. Use as the final step in a cloud IDP password-reset workflow.

params.name: idp
params.description: IDP identifier from detect_identity_provider.

params.name: tenant
params.description: IDP tenant slug (required for Okta).

tools.description:
Waits for the user to confirm an out-of-band action (e.g. 'did you finish resetting your password in the browser?'). Emits a UserAckCard to the renderer with a prompt and clickable options; returns the chosen option id to the agent.

IMPORTANT — this is a user-wait GATE, not a regular tool. G4 bypasses the 60s TOOL_TIMEOUT_MS ceiling for this call and instead races the user's choice against USER_ACK_TIMEOUT_MS (default 15 min). Include one option with id 'done' to proceed; other option ids (e.g. 'failed', 'cancel') should cause the skill to stop subsequent plan steps.

params.name: prompt
params.description: Short question to show the user in the UserAckCard (e.g. 'Did you complete the password reset in the browser?
